title: Intricately Wrapped Ukrainian Beef Dumplings
description: Earthly and crisp Ukrainian dish featuring baby lettuce leaves, perfect for a date night, with a low glycemic index for a healthier option
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, minced garlic, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the beef mixture, mixing until a dough forms.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and water, then add this mixture to the dough, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 20 equal pieces, rolling each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a baby lettuce leaf in the center of each dough circle, then place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of the lettuce leaf.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, then gently add the dumplings to the pot.
Cook the dumplings for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
